commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch is narrow and I found no discrete code defect; maintainers only need to accept the prompt redaction boundary and normal merge validation.; Cleared: The diff broadens Codex prompt context, but it preserves only tool-call input shapes and runs tool-result content through the existing forced tool-payload redaction helpers; no concrete secret-handling regressi... (sha=e3db9610349fef88e92accedf8c5872b600a6187)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server context-engine projection to preserve sanitized tool payload evidence and adds a regression test for per-turn projections.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: non-`thread_bootstrap` context-engine projections leave `contextEngineProjection` undefined and current main passes `toolPayloadMode: "elide"`. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch is narrow and I found no discrete code defect; maintainers only need to accept the prompt redaction boundary and normal merge validation.

target.title: Preserve Codex context tool evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Solution: preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections, matching the safer preserved mode already available in the projection layer.
- What changed: tool-call inputs retain only shape/type metadata; tool-result content is retained after existing redaction.
- Scope boundary: this does not change context selection/ranking; it fixes the payload elision once context-engine messages are selected.

## Motivation

Keshav pointed out that a later Codex turn did not show the earlier timeout-halving work or the 01:39 final answer evidence. The prompt only showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`, which made Codex reattempt work instead of seeing prior evidence.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85219
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex context-engine per-turn projections no longer reduce prior tool work to empty placeholders that hide useful evidence.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this host, branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, commit `e3db9610349fef88e92accedf8c5872b600a6187`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --eval ...` from the repo root to call the production `projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex` path with preserved payload mode and a tool call/result containing prior timeout evidence plus redaction-sensitive strings.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live console output from the patched checkout:

```text
contains tool call: true
contains input shape: true
contains useful result: true
contains elision marker: false
contains raw shell command: false
contains raw secret: false
```

- Observed result after fix: The projected prompt includes the useful result text `already halved timeoutSeconds=7200`, includes tool input shape metadata, does not include `[content omitted]`, and does not expose the raw shell command or raw secret-like token.
- What was not tested: Full production app-server turn against a live Codex session was not exercised locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The captured 10:18 local Codex session prompt showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: call_... [content omitted]` and did not include `halved`, `1:37`, or `1:39`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `run-attempt.ts` passed `toolPayloadMode: contextEngineProjection ? "preserve" : "elide"`, so active per-turn context-engine projections used elision even when the projection engine had safe redaction support.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression covered active per-turn context-engine projections preserving redacted tool-result evidence.
- Contributing context (if known): The preserved mode existed for projected context, but per-turn projection used the default elide path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: active context-engine projection preserves useful tool-result evidence while redacting raw shell command values and secret-like strings.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the Codex run-attempt prompt sent to `turn/start`, where the bug occurred.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `context-engine-projection.test.ts` covers projection behavior directly; the new test covers the app-server callsite.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex should have enough redacted prior tool-result context to avoid repeating completed work when OpenClaw context-engine projections include that history.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
